mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.5 percentage point threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding corporate output capacity and improving profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of more than 1% over 3 months coincide with negative 20-day returns in more than 65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% month-over-month are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth above 2% is followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing demand and deteriorating corporate revenue prospects, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines greater than 1.5% over 3 months are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases.
